statement: A fidelity-first L2/L3 prompt design with explicit non-expansion constraints will reduce verbosity drift on long survey-heavy evidence without introducing major factual omissions.
rationale: Judges converged on D-style hybrid prompts as best balance; falsifier still flags omission risk requiring gated validation.
keyAssumptions: Conditional long-survey mode does not over-compress distinct datapoints.
L3 one-to-one mapping preserves modality/attribution markers.
validationCriteria: L3 length <= L2 length for all canary examples.
No stage completion regressions in window orchestration telemetry.
critiquePoints: Current evidence set includes only 2 QA examples.
Judge disagreement existed in round 1 for L2 strategy.
questions: What is acceptable compression ratio for survey-heavy cases before fidelity degradation?
